Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.310, 583.320, And 583.360
Off Calendar May-18-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
James N. Sinunu of BERKES CRANE SANTANA & SPANGLER LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant Goodman Lumber Company.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Goodman Lumber Company's Motion to Dismiss is GRANTED under C.C.P. sections 583.310, 583.320, and 583.360. No opposition filed. Defendant's proof of service demonstrates that it properly served Plaintiffs.

As to the survival claims under Decedent's personal injury action, dismissal is mandatory under C.C.P. section 583.360. After commencing the action against Defendant in 2014, and accounting for tolling during appeal, Plaintiffs failed to bring the action to trial within five years and six months under C.C.P. section 583.310 and C.R.C. emergency rule 10(a). After the filing of remittitur by the clerk following Plaintiffs' appeal, Plaintiffs also failed to bring the action to trial within three years and six months under C.C.P. section 583.320(a)(3) and C.R.C. emergency rule 10(b).

As to the wrongful death claims, dismissal is mandatory under C.C.P. section 583.360. After commencing the action against Defendant in 2017, Plaintiffs failed to bring the action to trial within five years and six months under C.C.P. section 583.310 and C.R.C. emergency rule 10(a).

The Court dismisses the entire action under C.C.P. section 583.360. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A) (D) & (E)

Prior to the hearing the Court disclosed her relationship with the treating physician Kenneth Yamamoto, M.D., but found that it would not affect her decision in this case. No objection was made by parties.
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial is October 14, 2014 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last Day (120 days) is October 17, 2014. Last day for hearing motions for summary judgment/adjudication is October 8, 2014. Plaintiffs have stipulated to shortened notice for such motions. Motions for summary judgment/adjudication may be brought on 28 days notice, oppositions due 14 days before the hearing, replies due 5 days before the hearing. For any written discovery already served, responses are due 20 days from this hearing or within the time dictated by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. For any written discovery served on or after the date of this hearing, the time to respond is shortened to 20 days. E-service is considered the equivalent of personal service. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Further, before any hearing regarding a discovery issue, the parties shall have an informal conference with the Court. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 15, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 22, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on October 8, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 26, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 10, 2014. No later than July 3, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than July 3, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on August 20, 2014 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on October 14, 2014 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by October 9, 2014 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
